summary: The PoC demonstrates that a malicious 7z archive with path traversal in filenames causes files to be written outside the temporary directory, persisting after scanner cleanup.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction should cause a file to be written outside the intended temporary directory when extracting a malicious 7z archive.
actual_observed_result: The PoC successfully demonstrated ZipSlip. A file with path traversal sequence '../../../tmp/payload.pkl' was written to /tmp/payload.pkl outside the temporary directory and persisted after scanner exit.
verification_method: The run.sh script creates a malicious 7z archive with path traversal, runs picklescan on it, and verifies the escaped file exists at the unintended location after scanner completion.
evidence_produced_by_run: File was written to /tmp/payload.pkl outside the temporary extraction directory
File persisted after scanner process exited
TemporaryDirectory cleanup did not remove the escaped file
File content matches the malicious payload from the archive

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable commit, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
